#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>

struct NetworkPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string username;
    std::vector<uint32_t> scores;
    std::string metadata;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream << packet_id;
        stream << username;
        stream << scores;
        stream << hexi::null_terminated(metadata);
    }

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct NetworkPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string username;
    std::vector<uint32_t> scores;
    std::string metadata;
};

NetworkPacket deserialize_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    NetworkPacket packet;
    hexi::read(buffer, packet.packet_id);
    
    hexi::prefixed<uint32_t> username_adaptor;
    auto [username_len, username_span] = username_adaptor.get(buffer);
    std::string username(reinterpret_cast<const char*>(username_span.data()), username_len);
    packet.username = std::move(username);
    
    uint32_t scores_count;
    hexi::read(buffer, scores_count);
    hexi::read_container(buffer, packet.scores);
    
    hexi::read_null_terminated(buffer, packet.metadata);
    
    // Skip any remaining padding bytes
    hexi::skip(buffer);
    
    return packet;
}

struct PacketInfo {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string_view username;
};

PacketInfo extract_packet_info(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    PacketInfo info;
    
    // Read packet_id using span
    hexi::read(buffer, info.packet_id);
    
    // Read username using prefixed adaptor
    hexi::prefixed<uint32_t> adaptor;
    auto [len, username_span] = adaptor.get(buffer);
    info.username = std::string_view{reinterpret_cast<const char*>(username_span.data()), len};
    
    // Read scores count and skip the scores data
    uint32_t scores_count;
    hexi::read(buffer, scores_count);
    hexi::skip(buffer, scores_count * sizeof(uint32_t));
    
    return info;
}

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct MessageHeader {
    uint16_t message_type;
    uint32_t body_length;
    uint32_t footer;
};

MessageHeader read_header_and_skip_body(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::InputStream stream(buffer);
    MessageHeader header;
    
    header.message_type = stream.read<uint16_t>();
    header.body_length = stream.read<uint32_t>();
    stream.skip(header.body_length);
    header.footer = stream.read<uint32_t>();
    
    return header;

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>
#include <string_view>
#include <algorithm>

struct PacketHeader {
    uint8_t version;
    uint8_t flags;
    uint16_t packet_id;
};

PacketHeader read_packet_header(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    return *reinterpret_cast<const PacketHeader*>(buffer.data());
}

std::string_view read_message_until_delimiter(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    auto it = std::find(buffer.begin(), buffer.end(), 0);
    if (it == buffer.end()) {
        return std::string_view();
    }
    size_t length = std::distance(buffer.begin(), it);
    return std::string_view(
        reinterpret_cast<const char*>(buffer.data()),
        length
    );
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

bool write_data_with_check(std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, uint32_t header, const std::vector<uint32_t>& items) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    
    const uint32_t HEADER_SIZE = sizeof(header);
    const uint32_t ITEM_SIZE = sizeof(uint32_t);
    uint32_t required_space = HEADER_SIZE + items.size() * ITEM_SIZE;
    
    uint32_t available_space = adaptor.capacity() - adaptor.size();
    if (required_space > available_space) {
        return false;
    }
    
    hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);
    stream.write(header);
    stream.write_container(items);
    
    return true;
}

#include <hexi/hexi.h>

    uint32_t magic;
    uint16_t version;
    uint16_t flags;

    hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);

    stream.read(&header, sizeof(header));
    
    return header;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <algorithm>

struct UserData {
    uint32_t user_id;
    std::string name;
    
    void serialise(auto& adaptor) {
        adaptor(user_id, name);
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> write_and_serialise_packet(uint32_t header, const UserData& data) {
    hexi::allocators::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::stream_adaptor<decltype(buffer)> adaptor;
    
    adaptor << header;
    data.serialise(adaptor);
    
    std::vector<uint8_t> result;
    result.reserve(buffer.size());
    std::copy(buffer.begin(), buffer.end(), std::back_inserter(result));
    return result;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>
#include <string_view>

struct MessagePacket {
    uint32_t header;
    std::string_view message;
};

MessagePacket read_header_and_message(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    uint32_t header = 0;
    adaptor.read(&header, sizeof(header));
    
    const uint8_t* message_start = adaptor.data() + adaptor.tell();
    const uint8_t* current = message_start;
    const uint8_t* end = buffer.data() + buffer.size();
    
    while (current < end && *current != 0) {
        ++current;
    }
    
    std::string_view message;
    if (current < end) {
        message = std::string_view(
            reinterpret_cast<const char*>(message_start),
            current - message_start
        );
    } else {
        message = "";
    }
    
    return {header, message};
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/dynamic_buffer.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <algorithm>

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
std::vector<uint8_t> peek_data_from_buffer(const hexi::dynamic_buffer<block_size>& buffer, size_t bytes_to_peek) {
    // Get the number of bytes available from the current read position
    size_t available = buffer.available();
    // Determine how many bytes we can actually peek
    size_t copy_size = std::min(bytes_to_peek, available);
    
    // Create a result vector to hold the peeked data
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(copy_size);
    
    // Get a pointer to the data available at the current read position
    const uint8_t* data = buffer.peek();
    
    // Copy the data into the result vector
    std::copy(data, data + copy_size, result.begin());
    
    return result;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/allocators/tls_block_allocator.h>
#include <hexi/detail/intrusive_storage.h>
#include <cstdint>

template<size_t block_size = 1024, size_t allocator_count = 100>
using tls_allocator_type = hexi::tls_block_allocator<hexi::detail::intrusive_storage<block_size>, allocator_count>;

template<size_t block_size = 1024, size_t allocator_count = 100>
void enter_thread_with_allocator(tls_allocator_type<block_size, allocator_count>& allocator) {
    allocator.enter_thread();
}

template<size_t block_size = 1024, size_t allocator_count = 100>
void exit_thread_with_allocator(tls_allocator_type<block_size, allocator_count>& allocator) {
    allocator.exit_thread();
}
```

**Explanation:**  
The solution leverages the `hexi::tls_block_allocator`'s built-in methods `enter_thread()` and `exit_thread()` to manage thread lifecycle. These methods handle initializing and cleaning up thread-local storage, ensuring proper resource management for each thread using the allocator.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/allocators/block_allocator.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <iostream>

template<size_t element_count = 10>
void demonstrate_block_allocator_behavior() {
    hexi::block_allocator<int, element_count> allocator;
    
    std::cout << "Initial free list size: " << element_count << std::endl;
    
    // First allocation
    int* ptr1 = allocator.allocate(1);
    std::cout << "After first allocation, ptr1: " << ptr1 << std::endl;
    
    // Second allocation
    int* ptr2 = allocator.allocate(1);
    std::cout << "After second allocation, ptr2: " << ptr2 << std::endl;
    
    // Third allocation
    int* ptr3 = allocator.allocate(1);
    std::cout << "After third allocation, ptr3: " << ptr3 << std::endl;
    
    // Deallocate two
    allocator.deallocate(ptr1, 1);
    allocator.deallocate(ptr2, 1);
    std::cout << "After deallocating two, free list size: " << element_count - 1 << std::endl;
    
    // Fourth allocation
    int* ptr4 = allocator.allocate(1);
    std::cout << "After fourth allocation, ptr4: " << ptr4 << std::endl;
    
    // Check if ptr4 is the same as any of the previous pointers
    std::cout << "ptr4 == ptr1: " << (ptr4 == ptr1) << std::endl;
    std::cout << "ptr4 == ptr2: " << (ptr4 == ptr2) << std::endl;
    std::cout << "ptr4 == ptr3: " << (ptr4 == ptr3) << std::endl;
    
    // Deallocate ptr3
    allocator.deallocate(ptr3, 1);
    std::cout << "After deallocating ptr3, free list size: " << element_count << std::endl;
    
    // Fifth allocation
    int* ptr5 = allocator.allocate(1);
    std::cout << "After fifth allocation, ptr5: " << ptr5 << std::endl;
    
    // Check if ptr5 is the same as ptr3
    std::cout << "ptr5 == ptr3: " << (ptr5 == ptr3) << std::endl;
    
    // Clean up
    allocator.deallocate(ptr4, 1);
    allocator.deallocate(ptr5, 1);
}
```

This solution demonstrates the free list management by showing how blocks are allocated and deallocated, and how the addresses of allocated memory can indicate reuse of blocks from the free list. The output will show that after deallocations, subsequent allocations reuse the freed blocks, confirming the internal free list operations.</answer>

#include <bit>

        value = hexi::endian::convert(from, to)(value);
    }

2. **`convert_from_little_endian_inplace`**: Converts a value from little endian to native endianness using `hexi::endian::little_to_native`.
3. **`convert_endianness_inplace`**: Uses `hexi::endian::convert` to directly convert between any two endianness formats. The `if constexpr` ensures no unnecessary conversion when the endiannesses are the same.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/detail/intrusive_storage.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <algorithm>

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
std::vector<uint8_t> preview_data_without_consuming(const hexi::detail::intrusive_storage<block_size>& storage, size_t bytes_to_preview) {
    const auto* data_ptr = storage.data();
    size_t read_offset = storage.read_offset();
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(bytes_to_preview);
    std::copy(data_ptr + read_offset, data_ptr + read_offset + bytes_to_preview, result.begin());
    return result;
}

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
struct BufferStatus {
    size_t readable_size;
    size_t free_space;
};

BufferStatus<> check_buffer_status(const hexi::detail::intrusive_storage<1024>& storage) {
    size_t readable_size = 0;
    if (storage.write_offset() >= storage.read_offset()) {
        readable_size = storage.write_offset() - storage.read_offset();
    } else {
        readable_size = storage.capacity() - storage.read_offset() + storage.write_offset();
    }
    size_t free_space = storage.capacity() - readable_size;
    return {readable_size, free_space};
}

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
void reset_buffer(hexi::detail::intrusive_storage<block_size>& storage) {
    storage.read_offset(0);
    storage.write_offset(0);
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/file_buffer.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <filesystem>

std::vector<uint8_t> read_file_data(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, size_t bytes_to_read) {
    hexi::file_buffer buffer(file_path, hexi::file_buffer::read);
    std::vector<uint8_t> data(bytes_to_read);
    size_t bytes_read = buffer.read(data.data(), bytes_to_read);
    data.resize(bytes_read);
    return data;
}

std::vector<uint8_t> preview_file_data(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, size_t bytes_to_preview) {
    hexi::file_buffer buffer(file_path, hexi::file_buffer::read);
    auto current_pos = buffer.tell();
    std::vector<uint8_t> data(bytes_to_preview);
    size_t bytes_read = buffer.read(data.data(), bytes_to_preview);
    data.resize(bytes_read);
    buffer.seek(current_pos);
    return data;
}

void write_file_data(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) {
    hexi::file_buffer buffer(file_path, hexi::file_buffer::write);
    buffer.write(data.data(), data.size());
}

size_t get_file_size(const std::filesystem::path& file_path) {
    hexi::file_buffer buffer(file_path, hexi::file_buffer::read);
    buffer.seek(buffer.end());
    auto size = buffer.tell();
    buffer.seek(buffer.begin());
    return size;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/file_buffer.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <filesystem>

int32_t read_int32_from_file(const std::filesystem::path& file_path) {
    hexi::file_buffer fb(file_path, hexi::file_buffer::in | hexi::file_buffer::binary);
    int32_t value;
    fb.read(&value, sizeof(value));
    return value;
}

float read_float_from_file(const std::filesystem::path& file_path) {
    hexi::file_buffer fb(file_path, hexi::file_buffer::in | hexi::file_buffer::binary);
    float value;
    fb.read(&value, sizeof(value));
    return value;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/static_buffer.h>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
struct BufferInfo {
    size_t capacity;
    size_t current_size;
    size_t free_space;
};

BufferInfo<> get_buffer_info(const hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, 1024>& buffer) {
    BufferInfo<> info;
    info.capacity = buffer.capacity();  // Assuming static_buffer has capacity() method
    info.current_size = buffer.size();
    info.free_space = info.capacity - info.current_size;
    return info;
}
```</answer>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
std::vector<uint8_t> preview_buffer_data(const hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, buffer_size>& buffer, size_t bytes_to_preview) {

    size_t read_pos = buffer.read_pos();
    // Calculate available data to prevent out-of-bounds access
    size_t available = buffer.size() - read_pos;
    // Clamp the preview bytes to available data
    size_t preview_bytes = std::min(bytes_to_preview, available);
    
    // Create a vector to hold the previewed data
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(preview_bytes);

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/static_buffer.h>
#include <cstdint>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
const std::byte* get_read_pointer(const hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, buffer_size>& buffer) {
    return buffer.data() + buffer.read_pos();
}

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
std::byte* get_write_pointer(hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, buffer_size>& buffer) {
    return buffer.data() + buffer.write_pos();
}
```

**Explanation:**
- The solution uses the `static_buffer` class from the `Hexi` library, which manages a fixed-size buffer with read and write positions.
- `get_read_pointer` returns a const pointer to the current read position by adding the read offset (`read_pos()`) to the base address of the buffer (`data()`).
- `get_write_pointer` returns a non-const pointer to the current write position by adding the write offset (`write_pos()`) to the base address.
- The template parameter `buffer_size` allows flexibility in buffer size while maintaining type safety.</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/stream_adaptors.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <string>

struct UserData {
    uint32_t user_id;
    std::string name;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream << user_id << name;
    }
};

void serialize_object(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, const UserData& data) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor adaptor = writer.buffer_adaptor();
    data.serialise(adaptor);
}

void deserialize_object(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader, UserData& data) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor adaptor = reader.buffer_adaptor();
    data.serialise(adaptor);
}
```

**Explanation:**
- The `serialize_object` function creates a `buffer_adaptor` from the `binary_stream_writer` and passes it to the `UserData` object's `serialise` method, which writes the data to the stream.
- The `deserialize_object` function similarly creates a `buffer_adaptor` from the `binary_stream_reader` and uses the same `serialise` method to read data from the stream into the `UserData` object.
- This approach leverages the dual-purpose `serialise` method, making the code concise and avoiding separate serialization/deserialization logic.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

void write_container_to_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, const std::vector<uint32_t>& container) {
    // Write the count of elements as a 32-bit integer
    writer.write<uint32_t>(container.size());
    // Write each element in the container
    for (uint32_t value : container) {
        writer.write<uint32_t>(value);
    }
}

std::vector<uint32_t> read_container_from_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader) {
    // Read the count of elements
    uint32_t count = reader.read<uint32_t>();
    // Create a vector of that size
    std::vector<uint32_t> container(count);
    // Read each element into the vector
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < count; ++i) {
        container[i] = reader.read<uint32_t>();
    }
    return container;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

void write_data_to_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, uint32_t value, const void* data, std::size_t length) {
    // Write the integer value as bytes
    buffer.write(reinterpret_cast<const std::byte*>(&value), sizeof(value));
    // Write the byte array
    buffer.write(static_cast<const std::byte*>(data), length);
}

void read_data_from_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, uint32_t& value, void* data, std::size_t length) {
    // Read the integer value from the buffer
    buffer.read(reinterpret_cast<std::byte*>(&value), sizeof(value));
    // Read the byte array from the buffer
    buffer.read(static_cast<std::byte*>(data), length);
}

struct BufferInfo {
    std::size_t size;
    std::size_t free_space;
};

BufferInfo get_buffer_info(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    return {buffer.size(), buffer.free()};
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

std::size_t find_byte_in_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte value) {
    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < buffer.size(); ++i) {
        if (buffer.data()[i] == value) {
            return i;
        }
    }
    return hexi::pmc::buffer_read::npos;
}

std::size_t find_delimiter_position(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte delimiter) {
    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < buffer.size(); ++i) {
        if (buffer.data()[i] == delimiter) {
            return i;
        }
    }
    return hexi::pmc::buffer_read::npos;
}

bool check_buffer_contains(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte value) {
    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < buffer.size(); ++i) {
        if (buffer.data()[i] == value) {
            return true;
        }
    }
    return false;
}

    for (const auto& tag : packet.tags) {
        hexi::write_prefixed_varint(buffer, static_cast<uint32_t>(tag.size()));
        buffer.insert(buffer.end(), tag.begin(), tag.end());
    }
    
    // Serialize data size using prefixed_varint
    hexi::write_prefixed_varint(buffer, static_cast<uint32_t>(packet.data.size()));
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), packet.data.begin(), packet.data.end());
    
    return buffer;
}

CompactPacket deserialize_compact_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    CompactPacket packet;
    size_t offset = 0;
    
    // Deserialize packet_id
    packet.packet_id = hexi::read_varint(buffer, offset);
    
    // Deserialize tags count
    uint32_t tags_count = hexi::read_prefixed_varint(buffer, offset);
    packet.tags.reserve(tags_count);
    
    // Deserialize tags
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < tags_count; ++i) {
        uint32_t tag_size = hexi::read_prefixed_varint(buffer, offset);
        std::string tag(buffer.data() + offset, tag_size);
        offset += tag_size;
        packet.tags.push_back(std::move(tag));
    }
    
    // Deserialize data size
    uint32_t data_size = hexi::read_prefixed_varint(buffer, offset);
    std::string data(buffer.data() + offset, data_size);
    packet.data = std::move(data);
    
    return packet;
}
```

**Explanation:**

1. **Serialization:**
   - `packet_id` is serialized using standard varint encoding
   - `tags.size()` is serialized using prefixed_varint encoding
   - Each tag's size is serialized using prefixed_varint before its content
   - `data.size()` is serialized using prefixed_varint before the actual data

2. **Deserialization:**
   - Reads `packet_id` using varint decoding
   - Reads the tags count using prefixed_varint decoding
   - For each tag, reads its size using prefixed_varint and then extracts the content
   - Reads the data size using prefixed_varint and extracts the string content

The solution efficiently handles compact encoding by using variable-length integers for size fields, ensuring small values use minimal bytes while supporting larger values when needed.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <vector>

struct PaddedData {
    uint16_t header;
    std::string data;
    uint32_t footer;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_with_padding(const PaddedData& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    
    // Write header
    hexi::write(buffer, packet.header);
    
    // Write data length prefix
    hexi::write(buffer, static_cast<uint16_t>(packet.data.size()));
    
    // Write data string
    hexi::write(buffer, packet.data);
    
    // Write 16 bytes of padding (0x00)
    auto padding = hexi::fill<16>(0x00);
    hexi::write(buffer, padding);
    
    // Write footer
    hexi::write(buffer, packet.footer);
    
    return buffer;
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/allocators/block_allocator.h>
#include <vector>
#include <memory>

struct PoolObject {
    int id;
    double value;
    
    PoolObject() : id(0), value(0.0) {}
    PoolObject(int i, double v) : id(i), value(v) {}
};

std::vector<PoolObject*> create_object_pool(hexi::block_allocator<PoolObject, 10>& allocator) {
    std::vector<PoolObject*> objects;
    
    // Allocate and construct three objects with different values
    void* mem1 = allocator.allocate(1);
    new(mem1) PoolObject(1, 1.1);
    objects.push_back(static_cast<PoolObject*>(mem1));
    
    void* mem2 = allocator.allocate(1);
    new(mem2) PoolObject(2, 2.2);
    objects.push_back(static_cast<PoolObject*>(mem2));
    
    void* mem3 = allocator.allocate(1);
    new(mem3) PoolObject(3, 3.3);
    objects.push_back(static_cast<PoolObject*>(mem3));
    
    return objects;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_base.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstddef>

class CustomBuffer : public hexi::pmc::buffer_base {
    std::vector<std::byte> data_;
    
public:
    std::size_t size() const override {
        return data_.size();
    }
    
    bool empty() const override {
        return data_.empty();
    }
    
    // Helper method to add data (for testing)
    void add_data(const void* source, std::size_t length) {
        const std::byte* bytes = static_cast<const std::byte*>(source);
        data_.insert(data_.end(), bytes, bytes + length);
    }
    
    // Helper method to clear data (for testing)
    void clear() {
        data_.clear();
    }
};
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_write_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

std::vector<uint8_t> write_and_read_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& input_data) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_write_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    
    // Check available space before writing
    auto free_space = adaptor.free();
    
    // Write data to the buffer
    adaptor.write(input_data);
    
    // Create a read adaptor from the same buffer
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor read_adaptor(buffer);
    
    // Read the data back
    std::vector<uint8_t> output;
    output.resize(read_adaptor.size());
    read_adaptor.read(output.data(), output.size());
    
    return output;
}

std::vector<uint8_t> peek_data_without_advancing(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer_data, std::size_t peek_size) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor read_adaptor(buffer_data);
    
    // Peek at data without advancing the read pointer
    std::vector<uint8_t> peek_buffer(peek_size);
    read_adaptor.copy(peek_buffer.data(), peek_size);
    
    // Get pointer to the current read position
    const uint8_t* ptr = read_adaptor.read_ptr();
    
    // Consume the peeked data
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(peek_size);
    read_adaptor.read(result.data(), peek_size);
    
    return result;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_write_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

struct TypedData {
    uint32_t id;
    int16_t value;
    double ratio;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> write_typed_data(const TypedData& data) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_write_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    adaptor.write(data.id);
    adaptor.write(data.value);
    adaptor.write(data.ratio);
    return buffer;
}

    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    TypedData data;
    adaptor.read(&data.id);
    adaptor.read(&data.value);
    adaptor.read(&data.ratio);
    return data;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_write_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <algorithm>

std::size_t write_multiple_chunks(const std::vector<std::vector<uint8_t>>& chunks) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer_write_adaptor buffer;
    std::size_t total_written = 0;
    
    for (const auto& chunk : chunks) {
        std::size_t available = buffer.free();
        std::size_t to_write = std::min(chunk.size(), available);
        if (to_write > 0) {
            buffer.write(chunk.data(), to_write);
            total_written += to_write;
        }
    }
    
    return total_written;
}

std::vector<uint8_t> read_partial_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, std::size_t chunk_size) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor read_buffer(buffer);
    std::vector<uint8_t> result;
    std::size_t remaining = read_buffer.size();
    
    while (remaining > 0) {
        std::size_t to_read = std::min(chunk_size, remaining);
        std::vector<uint8_t> chunk(to_read);
        read_buffer.read(chunk.data(), to_read);
        result.insert(result.end(), chunk.begin(), chunk.begin() + to_read);
        remaining -= to_read;
    }
    
    return result;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstring>

struct Header {
    uint32_t magic;
    uint16_t version;
};

Header peek_header_without_reading(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_read_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    
    // Peek at the header data without consuming it
    auto header_data = adaptor.copy(6);
    
    // Get the current read pointer to verify position
    auto ptr = adaptor.read_ptr();
    
    // Construct Header from the copied data
    Header header;
    std::memcpy(&header.magic, header_data.data(), sizeof(header.magic));
    std::memcpy(&header.version, header_data.data() + 4, sizeof(header.version));
    
    return header;
}

std::vector<uint8_t> peek_then_conditionally_read(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_read_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    
    // Peek at the first byte
    auto first_byte = adaptor.copy(1);
    
    // Conditionally read more data based on the peeked value
    if (first_byte[0] == 0x01) {
        return adaptor.read(); // Read the entire buffer if condition met
    } else {
        return {}; // Return empty vector if condition not met
    }
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct ProtocolPacket {
    uint32_t message_id;
    std::vector<uint16_t> data_values;
    uint32_t checksum;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_protocol_packet(const ProtocolPacket& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::binary_stream_writer writer(hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>>(buffer));
    
    // Convert message_id to big-endian and write
    auto converted_msg_id = native_to_big(packet.message_id);
    writer.write(converted_msg_id);
    
    // Convert each data_value to big-endian and write as container
    std::vector<uint16_t> converted_data;
    for (auto val : packet.data_values) {
        converted_data.push_back(native_to_big(val));
    }
    writer.write_container(converted_data);
    
    // Convert checksum to big-endian and write
    auto converted_checksum = native_to_big(packet.checksum);
    writer.write(converted_checksum);
    
    return buffer;
}

ProtocolPacket deserialize_protocol_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::binary_stream_reader reader(hexi::buffer_adaptor<const uint8_t>(buffer));
    
    ProtocolPacket packet;
    
    // Read message_id and convert from big-endian to native
    packet.message_id = big_to_native(reader.read<uint32_t>());
    
    // Read count of data_values and convert to native
    uint32_t count = big_to_native(reader.read<uint32_t>());
    
    // Read data_values as container, each element converted from big-endian to native
    std::vector<uint16_t> data_values = reader.read_container<uint16_t>(count);
    packet.data_values = std::move(data_values);
    
    // Read checksum and convert from big-endian to native
    packet.checksum = big_to_native(reader.read<uint32_t>());
    
    return packet;
}

struct ProtocolHeader {
    uint32_t message_id;
};

ProtocolHeader extract_protocol_header(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    ProtocolHeader header;
    
    // Get span for the first 4 bytes (message_id in big-endian)
    auto header_span = buffer.subspan(0, 4);
    
    // Convert big-endian bytes to native uint32_t
    header.message_id = big_to_native(hexi::endian::bytes_to_uint32(header_span));
    
    // Skip the header by creating a new span starting from index 4
    auto remaining = buffer.subspan(4);
    
    return header;
}

struct MeasurementData {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::vector<float> temperatures;
    double pressure;
    
    void serialise(auto& adaptor) const {
        // Convert sensor_id to little-endian
        auto converted_id = hexi::conditional_reverse(sensor_id, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
        adaptor.write(&converted_id, sizeof(converted_id));
        
        // Write each temperature converted to little-endian
        for (float temp : temperatures) {
            auto converted_temp = hexi::conditional_reverse(temp, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
            adaptor.write(&converted_temp, sizeof(converted_temp));
        }
        
        // Convert pressure to little-endian
        auto converted_pressure = hexi::conditional_reverse(pressure, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
        adaptor.write(&converted_pressure, sizeof(converted_pressure));
    }
    
    void deserialise(auto& adaptor) {
        // Read sensor_id (will be little-endian)
        uint32_t raw_id;
        adaptor.read(&raw_id, sizeof(raw_id));
        sensor_id = hexi::little_to_native_inplace(raw_id);
        
        // Read each temperature and convert from little-endian
        temperatures.clear();
        float temp;
        while (adaptor.read(&temp, sizeof(temp))) {
            temperatures.push_back(hexi::little_to_native_inplace(temp));
        }
        
        // Read pressure and convert from little-endian
        double raw_pressure;
        adaptor.read(&raw_pressure, sizeof(raw_pressure));
        pressure = hexi::little_to_native_inplace(raw_pressure);
    }
};

Then, the serialize_measurement_data function:

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_measurement_data(const MeasurementData& data) {
    hexi::allocators/block_allocator allocator;
    hexi::stream_adaptor adaptor(allocator);
    data.serialise(adaptor);
    return adaptor.to_vector();
}

MeasurementData deserialize_measurement_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::allocators/block_allocator allocator;
    hexi::stream_adaptor adaptor(allocator);
    adaptor.write(buffer.data(), buffer.size());
    MeasurementData data;
    data.deserialise(adaptor);
    return data;
}

struct MeasurementData {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::vector<float> temperatures;
    double pressure;

        // Convert sensor_id to little-endian and write
        auto converted_id = hexi::conditional_reverse(sensor_id, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
        adaptor.write(&converted_id, sizeof(converted_id));
        
        // Write each temperature converted to little-endian
        for (float temp : temperatures) {
            auto converted_temp = hexi::conditional_reverse(temp, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
            adaptor.write(&converted_temp, sizeof(converted_temp));
        }
        
        // Convert pressure to little-endian and write
        auto converted_pressure = hexi::conditional_reverse(pressure, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
        adaptor.write(&converted_pressure, sizeof(converted_pressure));
    }
    
    void deserialise(auto& adaptor) {
        // Read sensor_id (little-endian) and convert to native
        uint32_t raw_id;
        adaptor.read(&raw_id, sizeof(raw_id));
        sensor_id = hexi::little_to_native_inplace(raw_id);

        }
        
        // Read pressure and convert from little-endian
        double raw_pressure;
        adaptor.read(&raw_pressure, sizeof(raw_pressure));
        pressure = hexi::little_to_native_inplace(raw_pressure);
    }
};
